review: My dad passed away back in May of 2024. We used to play this game together all the time. It was his favorite game to play. We were both excited when the 4th game launched and played online together against the AI all the time. He mained as the English and I mained as the French. My dad was retired at the time and pretty much played this game up to the day he went to the hospital. Sadly, we lost him about a month later due to cancer... He had over 600+ hours logged on this game. I still play this game to this day in memory of my father. I miss you so much dad!

A triumphant return to the golden age of RTS, polished to absolute modern perfection.

Age of Empires IV is a masterclass in how to revive a legendary franchise. It had the incredibly difficult task of living up to the monumental legacy of Age of Empires II, but it managed to pull it off flawlessly. This isn't just a nostalgic cash grab; it is a genuinely brilliant, thoughtful evolution of the AoE formula we all know and love. It keeps the core gameplay loop intact—gathering resources, advancing through the ages, and building massive armies—but it streamlines the tedious parts and modernizes the controls so that everything feels incredibly fluid and responsive.

Aesthetically, the game is an absolute triumph. The engine is gorgeous, and watching your humble Dark Age village organically expand into a sprawling, heavily fortified Imperial City is just as satisfying as ever. The animations, the destruction physics when a trebuchet smashes into a stone wall, and the rich, vibrant environments make every single match a visual treat. Furthermore, the single-player campaign is presented with actual documentary-quality historical footage, seamlessly blending real-world education with the incredibly engaging, sweeping aesthetic of the game.

Mechanically, it is the best the series has ever been. The greatest evolution here is how beautifully distinct the civilizations are. Instead of just giving every faction the exact same units with a different color palette, playing the Mongols (who can literally pack up their entire base and move it) feels completely different from playing the defensive powerhouse of the English or the scholar-focused Delhi Sultanate. It strikes the perfect balance: it is incredibly welcoming for newcomers who just want to turtle up and build cool castles, but deep enough to keep competitive RTS veterans completely hooked.

Un retorn magistral que et deixa amb ganes de molt més.
Com a gran amant de l'estratègia, Age of Empires IV t'ha semblat una autèntica meravella i ha superat totes les teves expectatives. El joc aconsegueix fusionar a la perfecció la nostàlgia de les entregues clàssiques amb unes mecàniques modernes excel·lents. La gran diferenciació entre civilitzacions, el disseny sonor espectacular i unes campanyes històriques amb format de documental fan que cada partida sigui una experiència totalment immersiva i addictiva.L'única "por" real que et deixa aquest títol és recordar els 16 anys d'espera que vam haver de patir entre la tercera i la quarta entrega. Després de comprovar l'altíssim nivell d'aquest joc i de gaudir-lo tant, tens claríssim que la saga està més viva que mai. Per això, l'únic que desitges de tot cor és que Microsoft i World's Edge no tornin a congelar la franquícia i que Age of Empires V surti a la llum ben aviat, sense fer-nos esperar una altra eternitat.

Age of Empires IV é uma verdadeira obra-prima dos jogos de estratégia em tempo real. Ele consegue unir o melhor da nostalgia dos clássicos da franquia com inovações modernas, entregando uma experiência rica, envolvente e profundamente estratégica.
O jogo se destaca pela atenção impressionante aos detalhes históricos, que não apenas enriquecem a jogabilidade, mas também transformam cada partida em uma verdadeira imersão em diferentes épocas e civilizações. Cada povo possui mecânicas únicas, incentivando o jogador a experimentar novos estilos e estratégias a todo momento.
Além disso, a qualidade visual e sonora é extraordinária: os cenários são vivos, as batalhas são épicas e a trilha sonora se adapta dinamicamente ao contexto, criando uma atmosfera que prende do início ao fim.
Age of Empires IV não é apenas um jogo — é uma celebração da história, da estratégia e da inteligência do jogador. Uma experiência indispensável tanto para veteranos da série quanto para novos apaixonados pelo gênero.

De grosses améliorations par rapport à AOE2 (gros fan depuis mes 8 ans et j'suis pratiquement en fin de vie là) ! En même ce n'est pas très dure d'améliorer un titre qui a plusieurs dizaines d'années mais chaque ajout est bien pensé et fonctionne très bien ! 

Gros points positifs:
- Le "réalisme" des maps
- Les graphismes
- Les animations
- Les ajouts tactiques des troupes et fortifications
- La narration des campagnes
- Les vidéos documentaires et les récompenses (qu'il n'y avait pas sur les précédents opus)

Points négatifs:
- Plus compliqué à prendre en main (mais ce n'est pas insurmontable) que AOE2
- Quelques légers bugs visuels par ci, par là

En résumé, achetez le si vous aimé les RTS, le médiéval, la licence, l'Histoire. Plus vous jouez à AOE4, moins vous êtes con ! C'est quand même vachement bien ! 

PS: attendez quand même un promo. C'est toujours mieux !

Age of Empires IV has some good ideas, and I’ll give credit to the built-in mod manager—it’s one of the better parts of the game. Unfortunately, that’s where a lot of the praise ends.

The controls feel clunky and restrictive for a modern RTS, and the co-op experience is incredibly frustrating. My friend and I genuinely spent more time trying to get co-op settings and shared gameplay options to work than we did actually playing the game.

To make things worse, some content feels unnecessarily locked behind DLC, which only adds to the feeling that the base experience is less complete than it should be.

AoE IV isn’t a terrible game, but it’s a frustrating one—full of good ideas held back by clunky design choices, limited co-op options, and systems that seem to fight the player instead of support them.

Age of Empires IV, strateji dünyasında taşları yerinden oynatabilecek kadar güçlü bir yapım. Özellikle grafiklerin netliği ve oynanışın akıcılığı, türün hayranlarını mest edecek düzeyde. Ancak Microsoft ve geliştirici ekiplerin, oyunun meta dengelerine ve özellikle atlı oyunlarına el atması gerekiyor gibi hissediyorum Fransız moğol gibi sadece atlı oynuyan uluslar çok güçlü oluyor erken oyunda tabikide pikeman ile engelleniyor ama uzun bir süre haritayı domine edebiliyorlar 

Tecrübe Notlarım:

Hile ve Rank Durumu: FPS oyunlarındaki kadar yaygın olmasa da, yüksek elolarda "map hack" gibi sorunlar can sıkabiliyor. Düşük ranklarda sadece strateji konuşurken, üst liglerde rakiplerin hamlelerinizi önceden sezmesi bazen sinir bozucu olabiliyor.

Stratejik Bakış: Bu oyun sadece asker basma oyunu değil, tamamen bir ekonomi ve zamanlama sanatıdır.

Yeni Başlayacaklara Tavsiyeler:

Sabır ve Makro Yönetim: Oyunun başındaki ilk 5 dakika, galibiyetin %70'ini belirler. İşçi üretimini asla durdurmayın.

Saldırgan Savunma: Tıpkı AoE 3 olduğu gibi, burada da rakibin ekonomisine taciz etmek en iyi savunmadır. Rakibi kendi bölgesine hapsedip harita kontrolünü ele alarak oyunu domine edebilirsiniz.

Age of Empires 4 is an RTS game that has two pillars at its core: competitive PvP and presentation.

Now that doesn’t mean that its single player isn’t good, it’s just that after putting many hours into this game you get the sense that it was made from the ground up with multiplayer as its focus. Be it how the civs play differently enough, how the map resources/exploration and the rock-paper-scissors gameplay of classic AoE is done to interact asymmetrically, unit balance, win conditions etc. And then you factor in the presentation of the civs, how each one has its own architecture and unit skins without sacrificing readability (a really hard thing to do btw), how many voice lines a unit has, and how the music that is unique for every civ that also changes depending on the Age mechanic or if you are in combat or not. This game has so many good things going for it, it’s absurd that they spared no expense and it shows.

That awesome presentation also trickles down to the single player campaign as after each mission you get documentaries about different things like how castles were made or what crossbow or horse breed did the Mongols use. They have insane quality and probably cost a fortune but are very much appreciated. Unfortunately the mission themselves while good enough to play are a tad bit forgettable as they lack character focus and they too play out as documentaries (which is boring in a way). I suspect that the campaign was used to introduce the player to mechanics and to generally show off the game and that’s a good thing, but you can definitely do that while offering something like the Fountain of Youth in AoE III. 20 years later I still remember it due to good characterization and voice acting even if it had a B movie plotline. So in the end the campaign is serviceable, which is good enough for AoE 4 as it caters mainly to multiplayer.

A none issue I have with this game is that water battles feel bland (and goofy looking) lacking the fluidity of land combat, but you can ban out water maps anyway. Also I feel like performance is lacking as I seem to have to restart the game after an hour or so or the game menu will feel unresponsive probably due to memory leak. Another issue is that 1/20 games will crash to desktop without even having a way to reconnect to the game so be prepared for loss of time and progress (which matters a lot!) along with rank (if that even matters to you) and of course, the game struggles a bit to run when unit count gets rather big - BUT I play on a laptop and the game DOES require a 3.6ghz processor so be mindful of that. Lastly ladder games are filled with cheaters. In the 3 months that I tried my hand on multiplayer I've been blatantly maphacked on, I've had people bug abusing spawning 30 units in a second and also had my civ pick swapped in selection (to a DLC civ that I hadn't purchased so that was funny). And lately the servers seem to be down half the time. But hey, name a multiplayer game without issues and honestly while my ladder experience was middling, I acknowledge that the devs do seem to try and fix things - like banning cheaters or patching abusable bugs - but are rather slow. Your milage may vary.

Even with all that I still recommending it though, as I believe the core of this game is just too good to pass up. The DLC also offer more single player (and the new one has characterization), more maps and civs and in general the developers move to the right direction with their releases. If you are interested in an RTS with incredible potential that also feels like a modern classic definitely try playing Age of Empires 4.

I have been playing the Age of Empires series for a very long time. I own every title except the first one, and honestly, Age of Empires IV is the most fun entry in the series for me. The gameplay, civilizations, strategy variety, and overall pacing are excellent. However, despite loving the game, there is one major problem that continues to damage the experience and pushes many players away from ranked matches entirely.

The biggest issue in AOE4 is the ranked matchmaking environment. No matter how hard you try to improve or play seriously, there are far too many matches where teammates intentionally ruin the game. Players frequently abandon matches, disconnect immediately after the game starts, use AI or automated behavior, or queue into ranked games only to leave on purpose at the beginning. This happens so often that it no longer feels like an occasional problem — it feels normal.

What makes this especially frustrating is that the developers have not implemented strong enough countermeasures since launch. Even if they claim there are systems in place, the fact that the same behavior continues so commonly proves that the current measures are ineffective. A competitive ranked environment cannot survive if players who sabotage games face little or no meaningful punishment.

AOE4 needs a much stricter penalty and reporting system similar to what companies like Riot Games use in competitive games. For example, if a player receives repeated reports from multiple different players over time — such as 5 to 10 legitimate reports within a month — there should be escalating penalties:

* Temporary ranked suspension for one week
* Then half a month
* Then one month
* And for repeated offenses after that, permanent bans from ranked play or even the game itself

A system like this would help preserve the competitive integrity of the game and protect players who genuinely care about AOE4. The people who would complain the most or quit because of stricter punishments are usually the same people abusing the system, trolling matches, or trying to cheat in the first place.

I truly love Age of Empires and have supported the series for many years. That is exactly why this issue is so disappointing. If AOE4 continues for more than five years after release without implementing serious improvements against griefing, intentional quitting, and abusive ranked behavior, then I may finally retire from a franchise that I have loved for a very long time.

先坦白，我是星际争霸2合作模式难民。蒙斯克大帝玩吐了，泽拉图旋转大脑也转不动了，于是被朋友拉来玩这个。一开始我是拒绝的，RTS嘛，不都那样。结果发现这游戏文明之间的差异是真的大，不像帝国2那种换个皮肤就当新文明了。虽然我只玩PVE当养老玩家，但这并不妨碍我沉迷到连饭都不想吃。

然后我奶奶也跟着玩了。 她现在农活也不干了，每天就在那里跑马，而且还不是跑肉马，一定要跑金马！我劝她歇歇吧，她说不行，金马冲锋那个感觉太爽了。现在我都不敢让她知道还有法国骑士这玩意，怕她血压顶不住。

说回游戏本身，这玩意是真的良心，买游戏送纪录片，而且纪录片的制作水准高得离谱，我甚至怀疑自己是不是误开了BBC频道。开屏那个艺术感，战役打完解锁的历史视频，我一个从来不care剧情的人都看得津津有味。就是教学关有点坑，中途退了得从头来，不过还好不长，忍忍就过去了。

新手引导这块我必须夸一下。作为一个文明系列玩家转过来的，我对"升本"这个概念熟得很。但这游戏的教学是实打实让你上手操作的，图文+中文语音保姆级讲解。最骚的是那个"战争艺术"模式，从经济运营到兵种克制到攻城，全都有金牌挑战，还有语音讲解兵种克制，长矛捅骑兵，弓箭射长矛，骑兵踩弓箭，这三角关系我现在做梦都在背。我为了冲金牌硬是练了一下午，第四波那个混合兵种把我打得怀疑人生，后来学会一字阵型拉扯残血才算过去。

金马是真的快乐。 我现在看见对面出弓手就两眼放光，直接金马骑脸，什么运营什么科技，冲就完了。但玩联通的时候就老实了，削成狗了，策划求求了给联通一点爱吧，我命官征税都征出腱鞘炎了，那点金还不够造两个火长矛的。那个朝代系统听起来很美好，什么唐宋元明随便切，实际上单挑根本活不到元朝，资源全砸地标上了，对手早就金马骑脸了。我现在排位都不敢拿联通，只在PVE里爽一爽，经济拉满然后暴兵，享受数值碾压的快感。

顺便说说那个观战系统，我跟朋友开黑的时候经常互相OB。延迟观战的设计是真的细节，避免了开图作弊的恶心事。而且数据面板一看就知道谁经济崩了，直接开嘲讽"你这农民数还没我零头多"。

哦对了，这个游戏的社区模组是真滴多，一百多页玩不完。我现在就想找一个平衡性mod，把联通加强一下，或者把金马削弱一下，算了当我没说，金马别削。

目前唯一不满意的就是别再削中国了，联通玩家已经快绝种了你知道吗？每次更新我都要祈祷，结果上次战国大名削了，金朝虽然小加强但那点数值根本不够看。中国玩家想玩自己的文明怎么就这么难呢。

总而言之，如果你是RTS老屁股，这游戏绝对值得入手；如果你是新手想入坑RTS，这游戏的教学也是业界良心。兵种克制不复杂，运营逻辑清晰，不同民族玩起来完全是不同的体验。当然，最重要的一点是，你得有一个能一起打的朋友，这是全游戏最高配置，比什么4090都好使。

P.S. 求求了给个免费的赞，我要攒点数换头像，这sb点数系统是真的烦。
P.P.S. 英格兰玩家别跑马了，你的后方是你的弱点，我已经偷过无数次了，感谢你的哨塔永远只造在前面。

This game is honestly a really great RTS. At first it can feel a bit overwhelming and even frustrating because you actually need time and practice to get decent at it. But once you start practicing and learn every civilizations it gets really fun and addictive.

You constantly have to multitask: economy, army,  planning all at once and thats what makes it really satisfying when you start getting better at it.

Its not perfect though. In particular, I think the history of Rus is not clearly separated from the Moscow state even though historically they are different entities with different development paths and the connection between them is more complex. I think they should be presented as separate historical developments.
Also matchmaking can sometimes put you against much higher ranked and more experienced players. Its not really a problem but in some matches it can be pretty hard to compete and you definitely feel the skill gap.

But overall if you dont get too stuck on those things the game is really enjoyable

现在并不推荐，如果说苏丹的崛起是最好的DLC的话，岳武遗书就是最差的DLC，半年的时间只制作出一个机制并不是特别有趣的变体文明，哪怕战役剧情可圈可点，音乐让人眼前一亮，也并不能掩饰这个DLC一团糟的事实，大量的模型是通过拼接原有素材组合而成，新文明的对战实力十分弱小，而且马农的采金效率吃不到科技的加成，更不用说还有新的提交bug，这个DLC远称不上合格的作品。
再说回现在的游戏环境，在某次更新中遗朝试图通过调整食物采集效率和减少初始黄金加快PVP的节奏，从那次更新开始遗朝对于游戏平衡性的掌握可以认为已经几乎失控，采集效率的调整迫使玩家开发出了背鹿打法,让整个游戏环境长期处于一种十分畸形怪异的境地,直到数个月后官方才进行了一次数值调整,通过简单的削弱减少了这种打法的使用频率.
同时现在金马以一种绝对的统治地位主宰了游戏的对局，哪怕是所谓的步兵文明，也不得不在对局中进行跑马.而官方完全没有进行及时的调整,哪怕是最新的补丁对步兵进行了加强,但是食物采集效率的缺乏任然无法消灭金马时代.
游戏现在的平衡补丁一两个月都可能等不来一个,每一次调整的质量也让人不满,很难想象官方在几个月内只能测试出这些东西.像红日这种堪称破坏平衡的文明在最近的削弱后才勉强降级为强力文明,可见官方的工作效率低下至让人发指.
总之,这个游戏现在并不好玩,它的帝国时代的那种沉浸感和二代相比并不强,不论是11还是44,游戏环境对很多人而言都算得上高压,pve的游玩体验也不如二代,新出的炼狱模式本来让人眼前一亮,但是在推出之后官方似乎完全没有对其更新的意愿,我们并没有看到另一个星际的合作模式在帝国时代中出现.一句话,帝国时代4最近的每一次更新都让人失望.
如果你是新人来游玩这款游戏的话,我并不推荐你为这样的游戏投资,如果你是老玩家的话,我也建议你慎重选择是否回坑,并且谨慎选择购买最新的DLC.

Moins original et novateur que le troisième ou Age of Mythology, cet opus renoue avec le gameplay classique du premier et surtout du deuxième opus de la série.
Ce n'est donc pas une révolution mais son gameplay apporte cependant quelques nouveautés par rapport au deuxième, comme l'impact des charges de cavalerie et les murailles qui peuvent être grimpées et garnies de troupes.
Les différences entre nations sont plus marquées également avec des bâtiments, des unités et parfois un gameplay différents alors que dans Age of Kings, elles ne se différenciaient que par quelques bâtiments et quelques unités spécifiques.
Le système de murailles fait ainsi vraiment office de murailles : il ne peut pas être fragilisé par des fantassins ; il faudra que l'ennemi débloque des armes de sièges pour les franchir ou les faire tomber (quoi que les béliers soient peut-être donnés un peu trop gratuitement).
La construction des campagnes est bien foutue, très immersive avec des vidéos de qualité et avec les interventions de la narratrice en plein jeu. Les missions plutôt bien scriptées ne provoquent jamais l'ennui.
Graphiquement, on aurait aimé plus de finesse, mais ça fait bien le boulot quand même. L'éclairage global ombragé par les nuages allié à l'ambiance sonore très réussie du jeu (avec ses effets d'écho ou des sons plus étouffés selon l'endroit où se situent les unités) restituent bien le terrain de jeu médiéval.
Enfin, les vidéos se révèlent assez instructives puisqu'elles se focalisent surtout sur les détails techniques des combats, faisant intervenir des passionnés qui font revivre les pratiques de l'époque.
Bref, la série des Age of (une série à laquelle je suis fidèle depuis plus de 25 ans) nous a pondu un autre très bon jeu de stratégie en temps réel à une époque où il n'y en a plus beaucoup qui sortent.

Das Spiel ist den Entwicklern sehr gelungen! Es bietet viele verschiedene Möglichkeiten, wie das Online-Spielen mit anderen Spielern oder Freunden, die ausführliche und schön gestaltete Kampagne oder die vielen Einstellungen, die man in diesem Spiel benutzerdefiniert gestalten kann. Ich persönlich spiele nämlich mit einem Ziffernblock, was auch sehr gut funktioniert. Auch das spielen mit Freunden ist immer ein großes Ereignis und, wie viele andere Dinge in diesem tollen Spiel, kann man auch hier viel einstellen. Von der Spieleranzahl zur KI-Gegnerstärke bis zum Auswählen der Karte und des Spielmodi sind hier keine Grenzen gesetzt. Aber kommen wir mal zu anderen Themen, wie die sehr gute und fortschrittliche Grafik, die das Spiel zu bieten hat. Es kommt natürlich auch auf die Grafikkarte und die Leistung des PCs an, doch wenn man eine Leistungsstarke Grafikkarte besitzt, ist die Grafik ohne Mäkel. Sogar auf meiner 2,5 x 1,5 Meter Leinwand erkennt fast keine Fehler oder Pixelige Stellen. sogar beim nahen ansehen.

Fazit: Es ist ein sehr gelungenes Spiel und eines meiner Lieblingsspiele. Alles läuft einfach reibungslos und das Spiel hat es echt verdient gekauft zu werden. Ich liebe es.

This game is THE premier RTS available to fans of the genre right now. The diversity of civilizations and gameplay styles is amazing. The attention to detail on the history of (almost) every civ and variant is commendable. The music is immersive, and now that I've finally upgraded to an overkill desktop after playing this on my laptop for 3+ years, I can confidently say the graphics are beautiful in 4k. 

The balance for multiplayer is tweaked every couple months and while never perfect, the devs are clearly committed to taking in feedback and making positive changes to support as many players as possible. If you like turtling and teching up, greedily booming, or full baboon all-in attacks, this game has a civ you can learn and find success with. 

If you're like me, and 30+ and miss the heyday of Starcraft, Warcraft 3, and other classics that we never realized we're so lucky to be inundated with, I think this is the best way to scratch the RTS itch and improve in a deeply complex and rewarding game. It's not a perfect game, but I can't recommend it enough.

賛否両論ある作品ですが、私個人としてはとても楽しめました。

過去作を強く推す声も多いですが、若い頃に夢中になったゲームの思い出は、どうしても美化されて残るものだと思います。
私自身も AoE2・3 を購入しましたが、特に2は4K化されたと聞いて期待したものの、グラフィックの粗さやゆったりしたゲームテンポが合わず、アンインストールしました。
AoE4に慣れてしまうと、どうしても過去作は“当時の思い出としては素晴らしいが、今遊ぶには厳しい”と感じてしまいました。

ゲーム性については、昔はNPCの理不尽さに腹を立てて、リアルでPS本体を壊してしまったこともあるほどでしたが（笑）、今作は手数こそ多いものの、理不尽さは少なく、落ち着いて楽しめる作りになっていると感じます。

不満点としては、キャンペーンとスカーミッシュのボリュームがやや少ない点と、アップデートの頻度が控えめなところです。
キャンペーンを一度クリアしてしまうと、基本的にはスカーミッシュが中心になるため、もう少し更新頻度が上がると嬉しいと感じました。

総合的に見て、AoE4は現代向けにうまく再構築されたRTSであり、私にとっては非常に満足度の高い一本でした。

Huge shoutout to the Age of Empires IV dev team — absolutely masterful work. You've managed to lock me out of online play for three days straight. I've tried literally everything: restarting the game, restarting my PC, verifying game files, even spinning up a VPN node in Seoul... nothing. The game still dumps me into offline mode the moment I launch it, even though my internet is 100% rock solid.
700+ hours in this game btw, so I'm not just some random tourist. At this point, I genuinely hope you keep up this momentum. Do the entire RTS community a favour and make this game completely disappear from the scene — that would honestly be your greatest achievement. Legends. 👏

Update: Servers are back, so as is tradition, I’m flipping my review back to positive. Good job, devs. Seriously, keep it up — like, actually keep the servers alive this time. I have the most unhinged love-hate relationship with this team. ❤️🤬 See you on the ladder… until the next mental breakdown.

Você alcançou a Era Imperial, mas a IA já construiu 3 Maravilhas, e navios de guerra da IA ​​estão à espreita no rio estreito.

Seu companheiro vermelho está quase sendo derrotado.

A notificação do sistema diz que a IA está destruindo o monumento do seu companheiro verde.

This review is just for people who enjoy PVE, I don't want to play PVP and I know there are many others like this. The cons simply outweigh the pros: Pros: + Fun civilisations that are unique from each other + Varied units even within the civs + Big maps and varied terrain + Terrain actually plays some part when thinking about ranged units of high ground ++ Art style is unique and honestly amazing + Fun campaigns Cons: -------------------------------------------------- AI is hands-down the worst I've even seen. I only want to play vs AI (no PVP for me) and the AI just knows how to build defences and skirmish - that's it. Because of this each match becomes; 1) build a good engine 2) build army up to the pop limit 3) run army into the enemy until your army dies (because the other side can just recruit locally) 4) repeat - No selective targeting. If you attack-move just towards the enemy base (not the best option but still an option in a pinch) the ranged units will fire endlessly at houses while the melee units will chase that one villager right into the middle of their base. Then, their newly produced units will just overwhelm your archers/siege because they're still miles away attacking houses. AOE2 solved this by having the shield (forgot the name) that limits how far units chase --- Mod support but only on paper, Microsoft keep changing the backend so mods become broken after each patch (even if nothing to do with the mod changed). This means that most of the mod store items are broken, except for SOME maps. Because of this, any mods which might solve the above problem (e.g. higher pop cap so you can just overwhelm their forces and base) don't work and the mod devs can't keep up with updating their mods when microsoft breaks them repeatedly for no reason. In short, all the pros are there for a good game, but the incompetent AI mitigates all of it by making the RTS side of gameplay redundant.

下面这个纯粹是写着玩的，大伙笑笑就好。

-----------------------------------------

金兀术率军出征，属下称岳家军骁勇善战，希望将军小心。
“撼山易，撼岳家军难！”属下如是说。
于是金兀术找到道家的方士，让他为自己卜一卦
推算黄道，星象，生辰，掣签毕，方士递给万户侯一张符纸
红色的“凶”字在兀术的手中分外扎眼，仿佛这不是朱砂匀成的墨水，而是无数靺鞨勇士的鲜血。
那些弓马娴熟的战士自他还在白山黑水间穿行时，自他还是猛安谋克时就伴随他左右，出生入死，他们的情谊已经超越了兄弟。
一滴豆大的汗珠从完颜宗弼的头顶流下，他的眼神闪过一丝惊疑，一丝痛苦，但随后便转变为一股愤怒。
“你......？“，猜疑的话语还没有说出口，方士先开口了。
“恕我冒昧，将军，符纸是竖着拿的。”
[b]“哈......哈哈哈哈！我还以为是‘凶’呢，原来是 [h1] ‘ 区 ’ [/h1] 啊！“[/b]

---------------------------------分割线-------------------------------

侍立左右的勇士亮出了朴刀，在他们看来，将军的勇武不容置疑。
手中的朴刀闪烁着寒光，就如天上的弯月一般寒冷
未曾想方士只是略一欠身，侍卫的刀竟落了空
[b]原来这朴刀过于沉重，女真人须[u]站定[/u]以双手挥动，即便在马上挥刀，也要先勒马再挥刀。虽能横扫，以一敌多，但刀刃太短，[u]攻击范围只有0.29，横扫的范围只有0.31[/u]，竟不及西方神圣罗马帝国大剑的一半[/b]
当女真武士站定挥刀时，方士只是略一欠身，顺势后退，便躲过了朴刀的一击
宗弼大怒，吹动号角，呼唤远处营中的靺鞨战士
不一时，数名骑着骏马的靺鞨骑手便驭马前来，手中的猎弓发出锐利的破风声
刹那间，箭雨袭来，方士无处躲避。命悬一线间，因缘际会，竟有一位僧侣持杖路过，说时迟，那时快，僧侣将方士护在身后。
“我佛慈悲，贫僧当以救人为己任”，僧侣如是说，“我虽不通医术，但能为阁下挡下这锐利的箭矢”
僧侣脱下外衣，靺鞨战士讶异的站在原地，[b]原来这僧侣竟是少林寺的武僧，自幼习得一门武术，唤曰“金钟罩”，刀枪不入，最锐利的箭矢乃至新式火器喷射的铁蒺藜，对他也只能造成一半的伤害，而僧侣只需稍做包扎，伤势便会痊愈如初[/b]
[b]反观靺鞨射手手中的弓，乃是狩猎野兽的猎弓，[u]伤害只有4点，还不如西方英伦小岛上农民闲暇间操练的长弓[/u]。打到僧侣的金钟罩上只有2点伤害，靺鞨射手们发矢如雨，竟不能伤其分毫[/b]。
“请随贫僧来”，方士随僧侣来到本地一座废弃的破庙，靺鞨射手们紧随其后，可是追到庙门时便犯了难。
”这算什么，纵火烧掉这破庙便是！“兀术不解，骑射手们只好道以实情，[b]原来他们是紧急应召策马而来，慌乱间只带上了寻常的箭矢, [u]无法使用强大的“燃烧箭”对其纵火[/u][/b]
"将军不必急躁，我们的工匠已经仿制了南宋人惯用的床弩，这床弩的箭矢必定能击破这破旧的大门“，另一位属下指了指远处[u]石墙塔[/u]楼上的弩炮。
砰！砰！，伴随着锐利的尖啸，两发如同标枪般巨大的箭矢破风而来，却只在大门上留下了两个鸡蛋大小的凹陷
[b]原来女真人仿制的这石墙塔既不同于泰西的巨塔，也不同于南宋的塔楼，部落的工匠仿制[u]床弩[/u]时，自作聪明在弩臂上刻下了两道箭槽，未曾想这弩炮虽有千钧之力，[u]可贯穿数人[/u],但因为装填了两发箭矢，[u[两发箭矢的威力都衰减到了原来的一半不到，一发箭矢的伤害只有20，而庙宇虽破旧不堪，但外墙却坚实如新，有五十的远防，弩箭砸在庙门上只能造成1点伤害[/u][/b]。
兀术无奈，决定纵火烧掉破庙。这时僧侣自知守着破庙不能长久，竟主动开门迎战，以拖延时间。说时迟，那时快，两发弩箭向他袭来，未曾想这僧侣只是站在原地，双手合十，弩箭便落在了他的身侧。[b]原来金人的床弩因为装填了两发箭矢，散布大增，且弩矢的直径也不得不做得[u]细如牙签[/u]，瘦削的僧侣只是略一侧身，便躲过了两发箭矢[/b]。
“我佛慈悲，庇佑善人信士。”僧侣微微一笑，“兀术！放弃吧。”
兀术看着手中发红的“区”,竟集火攻心，倒地不起。

欲知后事如何，且听（平衡补丁）下回分解！（乐）

傻逼游戏，兵种文明极度不平衡，部队操作手感稀烂，僵硬且极不灵活。寻路系统做得不行。游戏玩法单调无聊，除开天梯就没了，所谓的其他模式也是换汤不换药，看不出任何新意。战役冗长枯燥，引导差，没什么让人玩下去的动力。优化差，时常卡顿。最想吐槽的一点就是战斗平衡了，以前基本就是剪刀石头布的克制关系，没有什么操作体验。现在好了，变成金马克制一切。游戏中没有任何称得上具有操作感的兵种，唯数不多的操作就是拉扯部队阵型，但是你稀烂的部队操作手感也让这一点变得极度让人不适。打天梯打烦了，想打打自定义人机，还得靠金马，理了金马就不行。想玩玩其他兵种，就被所有人机联合起来干碎，后面越来越无聊。你们为什么连人机行动逻辑都做不好呢？我现在真心觉得这游戏全方面不如星际争霸2，还是回去玩星际2吧

The game is really well made! Colorful and, I assume, rather accurate people and buildings of each culture and time period.  Most of all, it is a lot of fun and every bit as well made or better than the rest of the Age of Empire series!!!

Games like this remind me of why I always loved RTS as a kid.. The visuals are simply gorgeous, and the documentary videos that go alongside the campaign are extremely well-produced and informative, I feel like I'm playing my way through a history education. And I've only just started playing this game seriously, I've yet to run any skirmishes let alone multiplayer skirmishes. Not until I've finished my deep-dive history lesson.

Basically, if you slept your way through social studies like I did, this might be the game for you. Not regretting spending good money on this one bit,

This game is AoE II but modern and better. 

An insane amount of QoL upgrades over previous iterations. Awesome environments, music, sound design and more... 

If you loved AoE II, then you will love AoE IV. Big thanks to the people that worked on this !!
